# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF TRANSFORMATION: God Momentum*

- id: 31981630

---

##    15\. "This Is What EVERY. DAY. Looks Like": The 40-Day Challenge 

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/2bseBkuSPe2K3jcqNO6Y_almahavingtroubleSMALL.png)

_"Getting started" is the hardest part._

Everyone waits for external motivation. But even when you get it-- it only lasts a few hours until the movie, or the article, or the podcast that inspired you wears off. Then you go right back to looking for a new "drug" to motivate you. 

That's not a way to live!

You could be motivated every day. 

By replacing motivation... With momentum.

Becoming the best at your craft doesn't happen waiting for inspiration.

Getting faster at making your art doesn't happen waiting for motivation.

You need momentum.

And the only way to get it?

You do "the thing you love "… every, single, day.

Any artist that's not creative every day will eventually start feeling incomplete. And at worst? God forbid...They'll feel like a fraud.

Are you ready to improve your speed, increase your output and make 'making your art' a 'rest of your life' thing?

The 40 day challenge is designed to help you do exactly that.

Whether you're starting a [DAILY PRACTICE](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/25245041)...

[DAILY PRACTICE](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/25245041): _15 minutes to 1 hour every day. This is for for students who don't have a whole day to dedicate to art. Like, maybe you have a day job, or you're in school or you have a family to take care of? Use the secret of daily practice to keep you in the zone. And to build up the habit of consistency. From now on you will make art (or do something for your art) every single day WITHOUT FAIL. With this alone, your artistic life will completely transform. And your mind will be more at ease._

Or doing the full on [GOD MOMENTUM](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/21444354)...

[GOD MOMENTUM:](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/21444354) _6- 12 hour days of full creative immersion. This is for students who have large chunks of time in the day. Usually they waste time trying to "figure out what to do". Or they spend their days procrastinating. Or they rely on sporadic inspiration. These students are ready to adopt the intense 'God Momentum' rituals to gain that momentum and fully dedicate their days to art like an athlete does with their sport. It's so intense it will require a sleep schedule, meal plans, Social media bans and cheat days so you never burn out._

Either one of these approaches will require daily consistency:

Knowing an instrument intimately... Making instinctive choices with brushes or colors... How to craft words to deliver emotion... Using premier pro to tell stories... Manipulating sound in Ableton... Or learning how to connect with your body to make it an expressive tool...

Consistency is the fastest way to reach mastery.

And consistency has probably been your problem for the last few years.

'Going daily' puts you in competition with the only person you should be competing with:

Yesterday's version of yourself.

  


# **40 DAYS OF DAILY PRACTICE WOULD BE KIND OF EPIC, WOULDN'T IT?**

If you've never made this kind of commitment before... Go slow.

Start with A [DAILY PRACTICE](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/25245041) (watch that module first).

Do it for 15 mins - an hour a day.

See if you can go for 3 weeks straight.

Doing it this way is like when you decide to move in with your boyfriend before you get married-- You're finding out if your passion / obsession level is compatible with the goals you've been bragging about achieving for so many years.

Prove it. To yourself.

But if you already know that art is a "for rest of your life" thing...

Then it's time to reschedule your life in a way that serves your goals.

Go for the full 40 days to achieve [GOD MOMENTUM ](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/21444354)(read Part I-VI first).

Just like the "Jesus in the desert for 40 days and 40 nights" story, when he found his true calling was to die on the cross.

Or when David Blain was suspended in a glass box in London for 40 days.

40 days is the amount of time that it takes to achieve that feeling when NOT doing what needs to be done is HARDER than doing it.

This incredible phenomenon is what I call God Momentum.

"Passion" won't get you to 40 days. Sorry.

To do 40 days the way I describe it in the 6 modules in 'The Book Of Transformation' requires an OBSESSION level of commitment.

I'm talking...

John Wick determination... Like someone killed your dog and stole your car.

Uma Thurman in Kill Bill conviction. Like when her body is paralyzed and she focuses on "wiggling her big toe" until she's a full-fledge warrior woman again.

THAT'S what this will feel like.

If you want revenge against all the forces that have been against you for so long...

And you want to say goodbye to laziness, procrastination, and lack of self-belief...

This next period of your life will be worth every bit of effort.

It'll be all-out war.

But the best part of this challenge?

Is that even if you stop cold after 40 days... You will continue to feel the rewards of having gone "daily" for months after the challenge is done.

Not a bad side-effect, right?

  


# **TIME TO EMBRACE THE ATHLETICISM OF HIGH-LEVEL CREATIVITY  **

Society has been pushing a romantic myth of artists "waiting and suffering for inspiration" since the year 1770, when that movement began.

And tabloid media continues to spread a lucrative fantasy of drug abuse as a conduit to great art.

I've dedicated my life to exposing how false this is.

The truth? The artists who keep releasing amazing work year after year are disciplined warriors.

Boring to realize, I know.

But it's a truth you'll need to swallow if you want to get into the big leagues:

"I'm gonna tell you what he does with his crew... They get up every morning and have breakfast together, and they talk about what they did yesterday and how to make it even better today. Then they go exercise together. They lift weights and focus on raising their 'chi energy' up. Then they get to the studio at 4pm. And they stay there until 4am. 12 hours! They go to bed... They wake up... And do it all over again the next day. It's this focused energy. I've never seen anything like that before. I can honestly say, it's not his talent that took him to the top. It's his focus." (RZA describing what he witnessed while he was in Hawaii for two weeks watching Kanye West work on 'My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy) 

  


# **ADOPT VAULT MENTALITY**

How do you become high-output?

You adopt 'VAULT MENTALITY'.

You become such a creative machine that it's necessary to start logging your output on a spreadsheet, labelling multiple hard drives and keeping a filing system for it all.

After a few weeks, you will have more songs, sketches, photos, paintings, videotapes of rehearsals etc... Then you could ever share with an audience.

Welcome to being a professional creative.

You're SUPPOSED to make more art than you could ever use.

How do you think Tupac, or Juice WRLD are still releasing new albums after they're dead?

The healthy creative's problem isn't CREATING. 

It's CHOOSING what to share that's hard.

The healthy creative "struggles" with abundance.

I want you to have that "problem".  

'VAULT MENTALITY' is what Prince had. He left behind a vault of hundreds of albums to release into the next century! 

That how ALL the greats create.

All of them.

With abundance.

The culture AND the fans cherish artists who create a BODY of work.

This could be YOU... But you're 'overthinking.

No more. Not on my watch.

Let's get you started on a journey that will gradually leave you no room for overthinking. That's the old you.

  


# **ACCOUNTABILITY PARTNER GALORE**

The worst part of daily practice is how lonely it is. 

That's why I document my daily online. So you know at least ONE other person is waking up at 4 a.m. on Christmas day along with you to "do 1 hour of freeform writing"...

You know how they say, "Those who can't DO— teach."

Please.

When the 'Make art not content' turned 1 at the end of the end of April 2021, I celebrated it by bringing 'Episode 14 ("Why Do The Same People Always Win?")' to life with the students.

I started recording and edit a one-minute podcast every morning.

That way you also get to hear clips from upcoming podcasts before they get released to the public; and probably some questionable stuff that will never get released too. 😂

You are part of that family of renegades now.

  


# **This is what EVERY. DAY. looks like. (THE CHALLENGE)**

Aspiring high-output creatives unite!

If you've been thinking of starting a daily practice... This is your time.

If you've been thinking of improving your speed, or increasing your output... This is your time.

If you've always felt like you needed some form of accountability... This is your time.

If you've been thinking of immersing yourself in your art with obsessive discipline... This is your time.

I'd love to see you raise the bar in whatever you've been trying to achieve.

Start with 21 Days Of 'Underthinking' to conquer consistency.

Or go full-on 40 days of God Momentum-- if you're ready to for full immersion towards your Polaris Star.

Whatever you think you can handle.

  


# **WHAT YOU NEED TO DO TO PARTICIPATE?**

5 Things...

  


### **1\. Pick your daily practice.**

**MAKE IT SPECIFIC. MAKE IT MEASURABLE.** "I will work on design stuff," is not specific. INSTEAD SAY: "I will master every parameter in After Affects by isolating one a day and make something out of it for 1 hour."

"I will write every morning," is not specific. INSTEAD SAY: "I will write 1,000 words every morning." OR "I will set my alarm and write for 30 minutes straight."

"I will get better at programing my synth," is not specific. INSTEAD SAY: "I will master every knob on my synth by choosing a different one every day and trying to make three presets I love by using only that knob. By the end of the challenge, I will have 120 new sounds."

**Pick your ideas, or themes, or parameters for each next day's daily practice THE NIGHT BEFORE so that you hit the ground running. Don't give your brain excuses to be lazy because you're trying to "figure out what to do" for 10 minutes before you start.**

Pick an activity that you know would make a severe positive dent in your creativity or your career—something you've been procrastinating or feel you don't do enough of. 

For example, ask yourself:

"What creative activity do I want it to "be like breathing" by the end of this challenge?

Is it writing a song a day? Painting daily?

Or take practicing new camera techniques so you stay fresh?

Or maybe it's mastering the software you use to make art? 

Or maybe it's the non-creative artistic boosters like:

_PSYCHOSOMATIC DAILY PRACTICE... Some creatives benefit the most when they get their minds and their bodies aligned. That's when all their creativity falls into place. I can relate 100%. If you're that type, then you may benefit from a daily practice of meditation, yoga, working out, and journaling.  _

Or maybe you've always wanted to improve your chops at making ARTISTIC, MEANINGFUL "content" for social media, but you're too slow?

_Pick ONE social media to get good at for the challenge. I suggest Instagram. Pick someone out there who has a great IG that you love. And every day practice by recreating a piece of content they did. If you do that for 40 days straight for this challenge, you'll become a wizard. And the great thing is, you'll be able to use your new skills on ANY other platform you choose._

_Or this one that I love that Marya did:_

_CHALLENGE: "Wake up early and don't touch social media for 3 hours." Uh... First of all... That shit is HARD. Especially if your phone is the first thing you reach for in the morning. Secondly... That shit will change your life. You will be so focused. And once you start "using" again, you'll notice immediately how quickly your focus deteriorates._

Or maybe you REALLY suck at promoting yourself? 

_Imagine you spent 30 minutes to an hour every day reaching out to people (gatekeepers, bloggers, influencers, brands, agents, and yeah even fans.). What do you think your life would look like after a month of doing that? If you recently released music, or a book, this might be the perfect daily practice. (THE ROLODEX GAMEPLAN: You spend 10 minutes racking your brain, looking through your address book, or through Instagram, and asking friends who to add to your 'reach out' list-- and then for 20 minutes, you feverishly write DMs and cold emails to those people.)_

A LESS OBVIOUS EXAMPLE FOR DANCERS, ACTORS AND OTHER PERFORMERS WHO USE THEIR BODY...

_Why don't you decide to choreograph something new every single morning. Give yourself 30 minutes. In the last 5 minutes, film it. Do it every day. But instead of increasing the time every day… Increase your SPEED. Work up to choreographing 2 things in that 30 minutes. Or work up to longer and more elaborate routines... But still within the 30 minutes. That way, 2 months later when someone's like "Hey can you come up with a choreography for my music video…" It'll be the easiest flex. You'll think back on your spreadsheet of 65 videos you already have collected in YOUR VAULT, and you'll be like 'Yeah, no problem'. Your self-esteem and confidence will be sky high because you've BEEN doing this! You'll be so fast now that you can come up with ideas on the spot instead of panicking and saying "give me a couple of days" and never getting back to them. You are now a creative machine. Professional creatives do hours, and days, and months of daily practice so that when we need to come up with something in 5 minutes... It's easy. No humming and hawing. No procrastination. It's now in our blood. I went from "knowing nothing about photography" -- to having "unethical marketing agencies trying to copy my style". It took less than a year, using this exact method. It's time for you to deliver on YOUR potential. Add to your vault of ideas every, single, day._

The point is to pick something you'd like to see yourself improve.

Something that you can realistically do every day.

Something that stretches your abilities beyond your comfort zone.

Something that you can commit to for at least 21-days... then eventually for 40. 

Ask yourself: "What would I like to add to my "vault" every single day? What new skill would make me feel like I developed a new superpower at the end of this challenge?

It should feel hard enough that at the beginning you get a pang of "Oh shit, what did I sign up for?" But by week 2, you're laughing that you thought this would be a problem... That's how you'll know how far you've come (And then we'll raise the bar again, just like I talk about in God Momentum part III)

### **2\. Pick a Friday to start.**

People like to start challenges on a Monday.

Rookie mistake.

Start on Friday BECAUSE the weekend is when most people have the most distractions... BUT it's also when your 'will to succeed' is at it's highest-- That way, by Monday, it'll already be DAY FOUR.

  


### **3\. Resolve to not fail.  **

Look, you may miss a meal (not recommended). Or you may get insomnia (it'll happen, but you can't hit snooze the next day... you'll have to suffer). Or you may even end up breaking plans you made with others (priorities, baby)...

But failing? That's not an option.

You need to make this pact with yourself now.

Understand that this isn't a challenge to "see IF you can do it".

Just like a marathon, you WILL get to the finish line.

What you wanna learn is what shape you'll be in by the end of it, compared to what you've been doing until now.

  


### **4\. ***Do Practice Runs Before You Start**

Test out your daily ritual to make sure it's manageable.

Are you giving yourself enough time to do it? Are you waking up at the right time? What time do you need to go to bed to make this work? What do you need to prepare so you're ready to hit the ground running?

Make a schedule / plan AND TEST IT OUT. 

If you're going for God Momentum... Get your VAULT hard-drives ready (main drive and backup). Label the folders you need. 

Just make sure you have a system in place where you can watch the abundance that you create accumulate. THAT alone will self-motivate you.

But PRACTICE BEFORE DAY ONE to iron out the kinks.

People like to "jump into it" without practicing. 

Another rookie mistake.

That's your ego fucking with you.

Somtimes it's because you have something to prove to yourself.

Sometimes it's because you have something to prove to the people who always doubt you.

Let all of that go.

Take your time to get it right BEFORE you start. 

This is a LIFE commitment. Let's approach it with care.

A methodical approach that will reward you later. 

It'll give you time to "mentally prepare".

You'll read more about this in: [HOW TO SUCCEED AT CONSISTENCY: A 40-DAY DIARY](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/31981735)

 

### **5\. Tag me with your progress**

Make sure I have your Instagram @ so you can see MY private daily stories as you do yours.

Feel free to tag me EVERY. DAY.

When you feel like giving up (and you will), I'll be there to keep you in the zone.

When you try to slack... I will call you out and raise your intensity back to your own standards.

I wanna see what you're doing. And how you're improving.

I wanna hear about what you're learning during these 21-40 days.

I may repost your messages at times to inspire others on the journey.

You will see me highlight moments and tips from everyone participating that we can all learn from.

So...

Are you ready?

Sometimes all you need is a start date.

Give yourself one.

Send it to me.

Tell me what your "daily practice" will be.

Make sure I have your IG @.

Let's go.

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/a17Sg9UQj61HmITgMtLw_william.png)

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

This last quote is so true. When you are creating daily, it is like you are being fed on so many levels. You don't go hungry and you're usually not sad either. But whatever it is that is in your way must be removed before you can get to the other side (consistency and high output, and hopefully high quality).

OK, Challenge excepted I am choosing God mode from this Friday April 12th 2024 - July 23rd 2024 103 days, I am going to do a project like no other i have attempted to do. I am going to do an anthology of photography work and the above dates are my book ends I have chosen. July 23rd because that is when we go into LEO and I want the project to be created and manifested in that energy. I am committing to 6 hours a day everyday until July 23rd with 1 day a week breaks for nature and mental rest. So here we go, I will share the broader idea as I continue to vet it out and refine it!!

Hello! I’ll start tomorrow 3/8/24. All week I’ve been prepping seeing how sustainable my sleep schedule will be. Honing in on what my daily practice looks like, what is art for me in this season. I’ve decided to focus on my voice, creating/recording mini sermons daily based on what I’ve been reading in Bible study. Creating titles and engaging questions to go with each sermon eventually turning it into a podcast or voiceovers for my collections. This aligns with me growing into a better storyteller for my fashion brand.

"Getting started" is the most liberating part. Changing language is so important. Some of the best advice you've given me is about changing the words I use. It's made quite a difference. Words are prayers.

My challenge starts tomorrow. I plan to do 30-60 minutes daily of pole dance. My goal is to increase my endurance to dance for up to 4 minutes no stopping, 15 minutes of conditioning my fundamental tricks, and set the groundwork for my twisted grip deadlift & phoenix by 1) holding my ayesha for up to 5 seconds and 2) touch the floor and deadlift up to starting position.

![Heirloome ](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/5c563af45e96599134e2a02fc2eee917?d=mm&s=70)

Heirloome 

Awaiting Review 

3 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/31981630/comments/19460790)

Starting my 21 Day Daily Practice Challenge on Jan 17th. Why not tomorrow, officially? Because I'm preparing and then practicing first to make sure it's achievable & measurable. I start a week-long harp-playing intensive this coming week (up-skilling with the aim of performing live), and then I'm travelling for a few days... but I plan on doing the Daily Practice during that time as a hardcore road-test. If I can do the daily practice during this 'unusual' time, doing it in my 'usual' life will be a cinch by comparison, and I'll know the Practice is the right one. Committing to 3x 'morning pages' & 20min uninterrupted/timed songwriting for 21 days, with a recorded result (even if its just on an iPhone - no excuses for being out of the studio), to accumulate a library of 'song starters', and to help hone in on the next direction of my sound. I figure that if something starts to consistently emerge & feels worth exploring, I should pick it & develop/run with it, and stop being paralyzed by all the 'what ifs'. I practiced & timed this today, and it took 45min including recording the result, so I'm allowing an hour every morning. Prep is to choose an instrument, sample or starting point the previous evening, and have it ready to use in the morning. Toying with the idea of having a 'pull from a hat'-style way of choosing the starting points so there is zero chance of 'getting in my head', but I've been digging Brian Eno's 'Oblique Strategies' too, so will roadtest both. I need to get better at 'underthinking' & high-volume output, so this feels like the right practice... at the end of 21 days, I'll have at least 21 song ideas to develop in my larger blocks of creative time, AND continue with the practice (or amp it up in to God Momentum if I've earned it!).

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Ashris Choudhury

![Zach Cohan](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/c2d9250fd643364855133bd765585ce2?d=mm&s=70)

Zach Cohan

Awaiting Review 

3 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/31981630/comments/19016698)

I had a limiting belief that I wasn’t able to be disciplined, consistent, or capable of doing god momentum. I quit my job working at a 3 star Michelin restaurant after selling my car and paying off my credit card debt and saving up a bit. I don’t know what the future holds but I knew if I didn’t leave I’d never make it happen. I’ve struggled in the past with discipline, consistency, focus, and follow through. I’d wake up at 430 am in college some days and sleep in until 2 on others. Im scared to fail but im willing to try again and feel more called to this challenge of god momentum especially now that im not working for someone else. I don’t want to regret missing time with family so I’m going home for my birthday and thanksgiving but will begin when im back on Wednesday 11/30. Bed time will be 9 pm. Wake up time will be 5 am. I intend to have a daily practice of meditation, gratitude, journal, & physical exercise before focused music making (writing, producing, recording) for 6+ hours a day. The extreme discipline is the hardest part. I’ve been criticized for behind too extreme/ too black and white but as much as many things are gray, I do think the difference between good and great is that disciplined obsession you speak of (Kobe, Drake, Gary Vee, Casey Neistat, etc. all artists I admire and am inspired by). I felt compelled to get out of the previous job and I feel compelled to begin god momentum, I’m excited. I also commit to make 5 creative marketing posts a day on tiktok to promote my music. As well as maintaining the consistency with my newsletter. I understand that if I sleep in or falter I will start over on day 1.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/31981630/comments/14412792)

Yes would love to follow your daily stories Father Bronques!! I'm at @nina_rubesa 🤗 I made 100 monologues during lockdown which helped me get over the need for perfection and commit to the process of writing and performing. It inspired the web series I just released and now after going through this module I'm setting myself up for 40 days of writing min 500 words on Substack which will be accompanied by a mini podcast episode too to increase my confidence in showing up as myself and sharing the way I see the world - on paper as well as through speaking.
